fields.title: Personalized Chatbot
fields.summary: An AI assistant trained on my personal background to answer questions about who I am, my projects, and my career story.
fields.description: Want to learn more about me? This AI assistant is trained to answer questions about who I am, my background, and my work — a direct, conversational line into my story.
fields.key_features:
Trained on personal bio and project data
Embedded directly in the portfolio page
Responds conversationally to natural language questions
No-code configuration via Chatbase dashboard
fields.role: Designed the chatbot's knowledge base, curated the training data from my personal background and projects, configured the Chatbase model, and integrated the widget directly into the Django portfolio template.
fields.challenges: Getting the chatbot to respond accurately and consistently about specific personal details without hallucinating or giving overly generic answers required careful curation of the training content.
fields.lessons_learned: How to configure and deploy a no-code AI chatbot, the importance of structured and specific training data for response quality, and how to embed third-party JavaScript widgets cleanly into Django templates.

fields.title: Multi-Agent Handyman
fields.summary: A multi-agent n8n workflow that automates job intake, pricing, scheduling, and client communication for a fictional handyman service business.
fields.description: This project focuses on streamlining data collection and processing by connecting standard office tools with autonomous AI agents. By leveraging n8n as the orchestrator, the system transforms unstructured natural language inputs into structured, actionable data.
fields.tools_used:
n8n
Google Forms
Google Sheets
OpenAI (LLM)
Gmail API
JavaScript (OOP)
fields.key_features:
Google Form triggers the entire pipeline automatically
4 specialized AI agents: Intake, Pricing, Scheduling, and Communications
Conditional IF routing based on urgency and estimate thresholds
Automated professional email generation and delivery via Gmail
Real-time data storage in Google Sheets Big Board dashboard
fields.role: Designed the full multi-agent workflow architecture in n8n, wrote prompt engineering instructions for all four AI agents, built JavaScript data transformation nodes using OOP principles, and configured the conditional routing logic.
fields.challenges: Ensuring each AI agent returned consistent, parseable JSON output — and configuring the IF node to correctly route jobs based on urgency flags and dollar thresholds — required extensive prompt tuning and testing.
fields.lessons_learned: Multi-agent orchestration patterns, prompt engineering for structured data outputs, how to apply OOP principles inside n8n JavaScript nodes, and how small workflow design decisions compound into large efficiency gains.

fields.title: Video Generation
fields.summary: A 10-second AI-generated video reimagining Gollum finding freedom from the One Ring, created with Google Labs Flow.
fields.description: A 10-second AI-generated video reimagining the moment Gollum didn't need the ring — built with Google Labs Flow. Part creative experiment, part exploration of generative storytelling.
fields.tools_used:
Google Labs Flow
Google AI Studio
Generative Video AI
Creative Prompt Engineering
fields.key_features:
Text-to-video generation from a natural language creative brief
Narrative storytelling through generative AI
Iterative prompt refinement to achieve scene coherence
10-second publishable output
fields.role: Wrote the creative brief and prompt, iterated through multiple AI-generated versions to achieve the desired narrative scene, and published the final output for sharing.
fields.challenges: Getting the generative model to maintain character consistency and scene coherence across a 10-second clip — the AI frequently drifted from the intended look or setting between frames.
fields.lessons_learned: How generative video models interpret creative prompts, the current capabilities and limitations of AI video at this stage, and how iterative prompt refinement is just as important in creative AI as in language AI.

fields.title: Campus Skill Swap
fields.summary: A community-driven Django web app where students trade practical micro-skills that universities don't teach.
fields.description: Campus Skill Swap is a community-driven web application designed to decentralize learning. While universities provide the theory, students often possess the practical micro-skills — from mastering a specific software to piecing together a complex quilt — that aren't found in a syllabus. This platform facilitates direct peer knowledge exchange.
fields.tools_used:
Django
Python
SQLite
HTML & CSS
Render (Deployment)
fields.key_features:
Skill listing and discovery by category
Community-driven peer exchange model
Full Django CRUD implementation
Live deployment on Render
fields.role: Designed and built the full-stack application from scratch — including the data models, views, URL routing, and templates — then deployed it live on Render.
fields.challenges: Designing a clean data model that captures skill offerings, exchange requests, and the relationship between users required several iterations before settling on a structure that was both flexible and queryable.
fields.lessons_learned: End-to-end Django development workflow, the full deployment lifecycle on Render, how to design database schemas for social exchange features, and how vibe-coding a user-first interface changes design decisions.

fields.title: LangChain Chatbot Agent
fields.summary: A terminal-based chatbot agent built with LangChain and Gemini AI that explains machine learning concepts conversationally.
fields.description: This project investigates how to develop AI chatbot applications using the LangChain framework. The agent runs in the terminal and uses Google Gemini AI as the underlying language model to answer questions about machine learning concepts in a natural, conversational way.
fields.tools_used:
LangChain
Python
Google Gemini AI
Terminal / CLI
fields.key_features:
Terminal-based conversational interface
Google Gemini AI as the LLM backbone
Explains machine learning concepts on demand
Agent-based architecture using LangChain chains
fields.role: Designed and built the chatbot agent from scratch using LangChain, configured the Google Gemini AI integration, and developed the conversation flow for explaining machine learning topics.
fields.challenges: Learning how LangChain structures prompts, chains, and agent behaviors required understanding a new framework abstraction layer — figuring out how to connect Gemini AI as the LLM backend while keeping the agent logic clean was the steepest part of the learning curve.
fields.lessons_learned: How to build a terminal-based AI agent using LangChain, how to integrate Google Gemini as a language model backend, and how LangChain's agent and chain abstractions differ from writing direct prompt-response calls to an API.

fields.title: Machine Learning Explorations
fields.summary: A four-model scikit-learn project covering digit classification, housing price regression, MPG prediction, and iris flower classification.
fields.description: A collection of machine learning experiments using scikit-learn's built-in datasets, exploring four distinct problem types: digit recognition with heatmap visualization, California housing median value regression, auto-MPG fuel efficiency prediction, and multi-class iris flower classification using K-Nearest Neighbors.
fields.key_features:
Digit classifier (load_digits) with confusion matrix heatmap using seaborn
California housing median value predictor using linear regression — identified median income as strongest positive correlation
Auto-MPG fuel efficiency predictor using linear regression
Iris flower multi-class classifier using K-Nearest Neighbors with confusion matrix output
fields.role: Built four separate ML models across classification and regression problem types, performed exploratory data analysis on each dataset, selected appropriate algorithms, trained and evaluated each model, and visualized results using matplotlib and seaborn.
fields.challenges: Working across four different problem types — binary classification, multi-class classification, and regression — required understanding when to apply each algorithm and how evaluation metrics differ. Interpreting the California housing dataset's feature correlations to identify median income as the key predictor was particularly challenging.
fields.lessons_learned: The fundamental differences between classification and regression problems, how to evaluate models using confusion matrices versus correlation analysis, how feature selection and exploratory data analysis directly influence model performance, and how to communicate ML results visually through heatmaps and plots.
